title: PCI-DSS 6.2 — Bespoke-software SDLC: code-review + SAST completion before CDE deploy
exclusions: Does NOT build or review the code; attests the required SDLC gates completed for any artifact deployed to a CDE service.
evidence: Saved search 'pci_6_2_sdlc_gates' (hourly) emits per deploy: artifact, commit_sha, sast_state, review_state, deployed_at, gates_met, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 6.2 Bespoke and custom software — gate adherence is directly evidenced.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Deploying a commit whose Veracode scan reports overall_state=fail returns gates_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A deploy with SAST pass + approved PR review is absent.

dataSources: `index=cicd` sourcetype=github:actions OR sourcetype=gitlab:pipeline; SAST output `index=security` sourcetype=veracode:scan; CDE-service lookup `cde_services.csv`.
spl:
index=cicd sourcetype IN (github:actions,gitlab:pipeline) event_type=deployment target_env IN ("prod-cde") earliest=-24h
| stats latest(_time) AS deployed_at latest(commit_sha) AS commit_sha BY artifact service
| join type=outer commit_sha
  [search index=security sourcetype=veracode:scan earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(overall_state) AS sast_state BY commit_sha]
| join type=outer commit_sha
  [search index=cicd event_type=pr_review state=approved earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(reviewer) AS reviewer BY commit_sha
   | eval review_state="approved"]
| eval gates_met=if(sast_state="pass" AND review_state="approved",1,0)
| where gates_met=0
| table artifact commit_sha service deployed_at sast_state review_state gates_met
description: For every deploy to a CDE service, assert that SAST passed and a PR review was approved before deploy. PCI-DSS 6.2 mandates a documented SDLC.
value: Replaces the pre-release tick-box with continuous gate-enforcement evidence. Auditors see the commit→gate→deploy chain for every CDE deploy.
implementation: (1) Route CI/CD + SAST events; (2) maintain cde_services.csv; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) gates_met=0 opens a security ticket; (5) pipeline policy revisions add gates. Indexes required: `index=cicd`, `index=security`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=github:actions`, `sourcetype=gitlab:pipeline`, `sourcetype=gitlab:pipeline;`, `sourcetype=veracode:scan`.
visualization: Table of gate-missing deploys, bar chart by service, single value 'recent deploys missing gates'.
knownFalsePositives: Hotfix deploys via break-glass workflow have an alternate review path — exclude with the workflow label hotfix=true when attested separately.
grandmaExplanation: We check on for every deploy to a cde service, assert that sast passed and a pr review was approved before deploy. pci-dss 6.2 mandates a documented sdlc. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for GitHub.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cicd` sourcetype=github:actions OR sourcetype=gitlab:pipeline; SAST output `index=security` sourcetype=veracode:scan; CDE-service lookup `cde_services.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Route CI/CD + SAST events; (2) maintain cde_services.csv; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) gates_met=0 opens a security ticket; (5) pipeline policy revisions add gates.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype IN (github:actions,gitlab:pipeline) event_type=deployment target_env IN ("prod-cde") earliest=-24h
| stats latest(_time) AS deployed_at latest(commit_sha) AS commit_sha BY artifact service
| join type=outer commit_sha
  [search index=security sourcetype=veracode:scan earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(overall_state) AS sast_state BY commit_sha]
| join type=outer commit_sha
  [search index=cicd event_type=pr_review state=approved earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(reviewer) AS reviewer BY commit_sha
   | eval review_state="approved"]
| eval gates_met=if(sast_state="pass" AND review_state="approved",1,0)
| where gates_met=0
| table artifact commit_sha service deployed_at sast_state review_state gates_met
```

Understanding this SPL

**PCI-DSS 6.2 — Bespoke-software SDLC: code-review + SAST completion before CDE deploy** — Replaces the pre-release tick-box with continuous gate-enforcement evidence. Auditors see the commit→gate→deploy chain for every CDE deploy.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cicd` sourcetype=github:actions OR sourcetype=gitlab:pipeline; SAST output `index=security` sourcetype=veracode:scan; CDE-service lookup `cde_services.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for GitHub. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cicd.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cicd, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **artifact service**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gates_met** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where gates_met=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: artifact, commit_sha, commit_sha], deployed_at, gates_met, review_state, reviewer, sast_state.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of gate-missing deploys, bar chart by service, single value 'recent deploys missing gates'.
